notes: Per-flavor chroots: publish submits each flavor only to the chroots listing it in 'flavors'. The MariaDB flavor is the primary RPM target (MariaDB is the default DB on Fedora/EL) and builds offline from vendored headers on every chroot. The MySQL flavor is built on Fedora only for now: on EL9 'mysql' is a module (mysql:8.4, not 8.0) and mysql-devel lives behind it, so MySQL-on-EL needs extra chroot setup and is deferred.

The engine plugin is ABI-bound to the server's minor version. The spec builds against the vendored source headers whose series matches the chroot's native server: Fedora 44 -> MariaDB 11.8; Fedora 43 and EL9/EL10 -> MariaDB 10.11; MySQL is 8.4 everywhere. The spec selects the MariaDB series from %{?fedora} (>=44 -> 11.8, else 10.11). Verify each chroot's actual server minor before a release (ABI).

No module_enable is required on the epel chroots for these builds: the MariaDB flavor pulls no DB packages at build time (vendored headers), and we do not build the MySQL flavor on EL. Enabling a non-existent module (e.g. mysql:8.0 on EL9) aborts every build in the chroot.
aarch64 chroots are intentionally not targeted: the vendored MariaDB my_config.h is x86_64-specific (type sizes / stack direction / endianness). aarch64 support needs its own generated headers.
